title: Annos affinity for the kana we and wo
display_title: Annos affinity for the kana we and wo
wikitext:
[]: Anno's affinity for the kana 'we' and 'wo'.             Quote Post by Namikiri » Fri Sep 18, 2009 2:00 pm I doubt this is of any significance,but I recently rewatched Gunbuster and noticed that the battleship Excelion also has these characters emblazoned across her hull. Sadly,I didn't take notes so I don't recall if it was "Wekuseriwon" or what...I seem to remember that the "e" was a normal katakana "e" and it was just "Ekuseriwon" on the ship,but I could be wrong. Anyway,it just goes to show that "Wevangeriwon" isn't the first time Anno has played with these unconventional kana. Maybe they have some specific meaning for him...?

[]:             Quote Post by CtHuLhU » Fri Sep 18, 2009 2:26 pm ゑ Elegant flourish as it is.

[]:             Quote Post by Namikiri » Fri Sep 18, 2009 2:59 pm Sorry,what is that? I just get a square. My laptop can't read Japanese. It's really frustrating when your computer can't read a language that the you can. It is,however,strangely satisfying as one of the rare examples of man being smarter than machine... >_>

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Sep 18, 2009 3:09 pm Namikiri wrote: Sorry,what is that?

[]:             Quote Post by Namikiri » Fri Sep 18, 2009 3:19 pm Thankyou much. One of these days I'm going to get around to finding that disc I need to install East-Asian languages on this beast-machine. Eastasia...? We've always been at war with Eastasia... Oh,sorry. Such a scatterbrain.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Fri Sep 18, 2009 3:40 pm You don't need any disc, if you're using windows xp just select the languages you want in control panel, region, etc.

Article: Annos affinity for the kana we and wo
[]: Anno's affinity for the kana 'we' and 'wo'.             Quote Post by Namikiri » Fri Sep 18, 2009 2:00 pm I doubt this is of any significance,but I recently rewatched Gunbuster and noticed that the battleship Excelion also has these characters emblazoned across her hull. Sadly,I didn't take notes so I don't recall if it was "Wekuseriwon" or what...I seem to remember that the "e" was a normal katakana "e" and it was just "Ekuseriwon" on the ship,but I could be wrong. Anyway,it just goes to show that "Wevangeriwon" isn't the first time Anno has played with these unconventional kana. Maybe they have some specific meaning for him...?
